bio.quote: Llewyellen "Lew" Tosferd, called The Finder, is a man who trades in lost things. Old contracts, informant networks, and a knack for being where information flows—Lew finds what others cannot. He is fr

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

novice.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

novice.description: When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

system.description:
A community raised around geothermal vents and heated gardens, where stewardship, ritual argument, and careful resource-sharing are survival skills.

Language: Sangøymål

Environment: Secluded; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Academic

system.description:
Skills: Sneak, Persuasive, Vigilant.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Forgettable Face*. Once per session, you can avoid being recognized in a crowd unless you make a scene or draw sustained attention.

novice.description:

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Forgettable Face*. Once per session, you can avoid being recognized in a crowd unless you make a scene or draw sustained attention.

system.description: You can don or remove a disguise as part of any test you make using the Hide skill, or while using the Hide free action.

novice.description: You can don or remove a disguise as part of any test you make using the Hide skill, or while using the Hide free action.

system.description:
Subtlety is your art, the tip of the blade your brush. You studied at a secret college, specializing in alchemy, illusion, or shadow-magics. Your training and knowledge place you among the elite ranks of assassins, spies, and commandos. But more potent than any weapon or sorcery is your insight into your enemies’ weaknesses.

As a shadow, you possess abilities that deal significant damage, enable you to move swiftly across the battlefield and evade hazards, and allow you to fade from notice even in the midst of the most intense combat encounters. You also possess more skills than any other hero.

novice.description:
Subtlety is your art, the tip of the blade your brush. You studied at a secret college, specializing in alchemy, illusion, or shadow-magics. Your training and knowledge place you among the elite ranks of assassins, spies, and commandos. But more potent than any weapon or sorcery is your insight into your enemies’ weaknesses.

As a shadow, you possess abilities that deal significant damage, enable you to move swiftly across the battlefield and evade hazards, and allow you to fade from notice even in the midst of the most intense combat encounters. You also possess more skills than any other hero.

system.description:
By observing your enemy, you learn how to use their weaknesses against them, building up a Heroic Resource called insight.

Vigilant in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain insight equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 insight.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you deal damage incorporating 1 or more surges, you gain 1 insight.

Whenever you use a heroic ability that makes use of a attribute test, that ability costs 1 fewer insight if you have an advantage (second chance) or double advantage (second chance) on it. If the ability has multiple targets, the cost is reduced even if the ability gains an advantage (second chance) or has a double advantage (second chance) against only one target.

You lose any remaining insight at the end of the encounter.

Vigilant Outside of Combat

Although you can’t gain insight outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost insight without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs insight, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited insight on its effect, such as Black Ash Teleport, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of insight equal to your Milestones.

system.description: While conversing with any creature you share a language with, you can decide whether anyone else can perceive what you’re conveying, even while yelling

novice.description: While conversing with any creature you share a language with, you can decide whether anyone else can perceive what you’re conveying, even while yelling

system.description:
Your sense for stealth shows those around you how to evade notice. While you are hidden, enemies take a disadvantage (second chance) on tests made to search for you or other hidden creatures within 10 meters of you.

Additionally, you have the Night Watch triggered ability.

novice.description:
Your sense for stealth shows those around you how to evade notice. While you are hidden, enemies take a disadvantage (second chance) on tests made to search for you or other hidden creatures within 10 meters of you.

Additionally, you have the Night Watch triggered ability.

system.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage incorporating 1 or more surges, you gain 2 insight instead of 1.

novice.description: The first time each combat round that you deal damage incorporating 1 or more surges, you gain 2 insight instead of 1.

system.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

novice.description: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

system.description: Whenever you make a test to read a person and obtain a tier 3 outcome, you notice several tells that give away their true feelings. Any test you make to read that person in the future gains an advantage (second chance).

novice.description: Whenever you make a test to read a person and obtain a tier 3 outcome, you notice several tells that give away their true feelings. Any test you make to read that person in the future gains an advantage (second chance).

system.description: When you make a project roll using a skill from the crafting skill group, you can spend a Experience to make another project roll for the same project as part of the same extended rest activity. You can’t use this perk more than once per extended rest.

novice.description: When you make a project roll using a skill from the crafting skill group, you can spend a Experience to make another project roll for the same project as part of the same extended rest activity. You can’t use this perk more than once per extended rest.

system.description: The College of Caustic Alchemy teaches its students recipes for the acids, bombs, and poisons used in their grim work. Graduates of the college are exceptional assassins. You have the Alchemy skill.

novice.description: The College of Caustic Alchemy teaches its students recipes for the acids, bombs, and poisons used in their grim work. Graduates of the college are exceptional assassins. You have the Alchemy skill.

system.description: You always carry a supply of smoke bombs to use for distractions and easy getaways. You can use the Hide free action even if you are observed and don’t initially have cover or concealment. When you do so, you can shift a number of meters equal to your Agility score. If you end this movement with cover or concealment, you are automatically hidden.

novice.description: You always carry a supply of smoke bombs to use for distractions and easy getaways. You can use the Hide free action even if you are observed and don’t initially have cover or concealment. When you do so, you can shift a number of meters equal to your Agility score. If you end this movement with cover or concealment, you are automatically hidden.

system.description: You know just where to cut your enemies. Whenever you make a strike that has no disadvantage (second chance) or double disadvantage (second chance), and that incorporates 1 or more surges, you {Gain 1 Additional Surge} that you can use only on that strike.

novice.description: You know just where to cut your enemies. Whenever you make a strike that has no disadvantage (second chance) or double disadvantage (second chance), and that incorporates 1 or more surges, you {Gain 1 Additional Surge} that you can use only on that strike.

items.name: You Were Watching the Wrong One
